name: Ndonga
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Ndonga is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Ndonga (Oshindonga) is a Bantu (Atlantic-Congo) language of Namibia and Angola, one of the two standardised Ovambo varieties (with Kwanyama), written in the Latin script. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script Oshindonga orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode ndon1254), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode ndon1254.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
